On August 20, 1968, the incredibly massive, historically definitive, and geopolitically terrifying Warsaw Pact invasion of Czechoslovakia officially and aggressively commenced. This massive, brutal military intervention aggressively annihilated Alexander Dubček's incredibly popular, democratic reforms, heavily proving the absolute, uncompromising rigidity of Soviet totalitarianism and deeply traumatizing Eastern Europe for decades.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On August 20, 1944, Rajiv Gandhi, the incredibly massive, universally iconic, and highly influential future 6th Prime Minister of India, was officially born in Bombay (Mumbai). The eldest son of Indira Gandhi and the massive, reluctant political heir to the powerful Nehru-Gandhi dynasty, he was aggressively thrust into absolute national leadership following his mother's horrific assassination. His massive, highly modernized, and incredibly tragic tenure heavily defined the massive technological integration and absolute, deeply volatile geopolitical struggles of 1980s India.

On August 20, 1979, the incredibly massive, deeply revered, and historically monumental Indian Prime Minister, Chaudhary Charan Singh, was aggressively and officially forced to resign. Heavily serving for a mere 24 days without ever facing Parliament, his incredibly brief, massive tenure absolutely collapsed when Indira Gandhi fiercely and aggressively withdrew the massive, critical support of her Congress party. This massive, highly volatile political maneuvering heavily exposed the absolute, extreme fragility of anti-Congress coalition governments during that deeply tumultuous era.
